title: identity — helper functions
description: One global helper. Everything else the module exposes is behind DI / facades — helpers here are for the rare case where a downstream module needs a synchronous lookup without pulling in the repository binding.

helpers.purpose: Case-insensitive email lookup returning the resolved Identity model (or null when not found). Runs through the `idn:lookup:email:{email_hash}` cache. Does NOT expose credentials — the caller may only read the model attributes classified `internal`.
behaviours: Returns null on unknown email (never throws — caller decides how to signal)
Returns null when the Identity is soft-deleted (i.e. the erasure hold-period is in effect)
Does NOT decrement the rate-limit counter — that's the middleware's job
Never logs the email as a label; only the fingerprint (SHA-256) if logging is required

helpers.purpose: Compute the canonical email fingerprint (SHA-256 of the normalised email) used across events, metrics, and analytics.
behaviours: Normalises the email (lowercase, trim) before hashing — same normalisation as CITEXT
Never reversible — no lookup path from fingerprint → email exists

helpers.purpose: Convenience accessor. When Auth resolves a User (via sanctum) OR a PlatformUser (via platform_admin), returns the corresponding Identity row. Returns null for unauthenticated requests OR ServiceAccount principals.
behaviours: Cached in the request-scoped container — repeated calls in one request don't hit the DB
Returns null on Web-worker / CLI contexts where auth() is null

notes: Helpers are registered by the composer autoloader from `src/Support/helpers.php`. Namespaced functions are safe to call across every framework layer including migrations + seeders.

None of these helpers accept a raw ULID as input — caller code that has a ULID should use `IdentityRepository::findById($id)` instead. The email-based path is the frequent one; the ID-based path is a code-level operation that deserves the explicit repository call.
